title: Production Go/No-Go v5
decision_rationale: GL-217 consolidates the post-GL-214/215/216 production-readiness posture, updates the blocker matrix and risk register, and provides conservative go/no-go decisions. GL-214 made narrow IAM improvements (role vocabulary constraint, operator create/revoke audit events). GL-215 made narrow tenant improvements (secondary route hardening, cross-tenant mutation denial). GL-216 consolidated operations posture documentation and added a local-only gate script. The GL-216 post-merge suite shows 0 real regressions. Multiple P0 blockers remain. No production SaaS readiness is claimed. All prior safety boundaries are preserved.

current_state_summary.developer_preview_controlled_preview: GrantLayer is Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries and synthetic/demo data only.
baseline_controls: operator token hashing PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 and tenant binding (GL-206)
admin token constant-time comparison and fail-closed route guards (GL-201, GL-206)
operator role vocabulary constrained to owner/grant_admin/auditor (GL-214)
durable audit-chain events for operator create/revoke (GL-214)
tenant-filtered primary and secondary execution-derived routes (GL-215)
cross-tenant direct-ID mutation denied on demo path (GL-215)
safe synthetic backup/restore and audit export check scripts (GL-205, GL-209)
ephemeral live PostgreSQL validation passed with synthetic/demo data (GL-206B)
structured logging, correlation IDs, redaction helpers (GL-208)

developer_preview.rationale: Clean baseline; examples deterministic; feedback routing established; GL-214/215/216 merged without real regressions

controlled_external_technical_review.rationale: GL-212 gate valid; synthetic/demo data only; no real customer/grant/institutional data; security reports via GitHub Security Advisories; NDA required

synthetic_demo_controlled_pilot.rationale: Allowed if synthetic/demo data only, agreement in place, no real data, no public publish, no production access

public_snapshot_preparation.rationale: GL-212 gate confirmed proceed-with-cautions; requires separate export/safety issue and explicit human approval before any public action

public_website_publish.rationale: No separate public publish gate has been opened or completed; must be a separate explicit approval

official_sdk_package.rationale: GL-203C boundary preserved; no package publishing metadata; no PyPI/npm publish

real_customer_data.rationale: PB-001, PB-002, PB-003, PB-006, PB-009, PB-011, PB-012 unresolved

private_grant_institutional_data.rationale: Same blockers as real customer data; no data governance or legal readiness

production_saas.rationale: Multiple P0 blockers unresolved across IAM, tenant/workspace, PostgreSQL, backup/DR, observability, incident response, deployment, compliance, and data governance

compliance_certification.rationale: No compliance assessment, DPA, or certification process started; GDPR/SOC2/ISO/enterprise readiness not claimed

live_postgres_production_readiness.rationale: GL-206B ephemeral validation passed with synthetic/demo data only; production DB topology, pooling, permissions, failover, backup automation, and DR not established

production_saas_decision.rationale: Multiple unresolved P0 blockers across IAM, tenant/workspace, PostgreSQL operations, backup/DR, observability, incident response, deployment, compliance, and data governance prevent Production SaaS readiness
remaining_p0_blockers: PB-002: Tenant/workspace production isolation not complete
PB-003: Production PostgreSQL operations not established
PB-010: Rate limiting/abuse protection not production-grade

real_customer_data_decision.rationale: PB-001, PB-002, PB-003, PB-006, PB-009, PB-011, and PB-012 unresolved; no production IAM, RLS, compliance, or legal readiness

private_grant_institutional_data_decisio.rationale: Same blockers as real customer data; additional data governance, legal, and audit readiness not present

conditions: no real customer, private grant, or institutional data
reviewers bound by NDA or equivalent agreement
security-sensitive findings routed to GitHub Security Advisories only
no real production secrets, credentials, or external hostnames shared
no public publish of reviewer findings or exported artifacts without separate explicit approval gate

public_snapshot_public_publish_decision.rationale: Public snapshot preparation requires a separate export/safety issue and explicit human approval. Public publish has no open gate in GL-217 and remains no-go.

official_sdk_package_decision.rationale: GL-203C SDK prototype packaging boundary preserved; no package publishing metadata, PyPI, npm, or official SDK/package release added or claimed

live_postgres_readiness_decision.rationale: GL-206B ephemeral validation passed with synthetic/demo data only; production database operating model not established

iam_operator_readiness_assessment.remaining_p0_gap: single static admin token; no OAuth/JWT/OIDC/SSO/MFA; no external IAM provider; no automated token lifecycle

tenant_workspace_readiness_assessment.tenant_derivation: server-derived from operator row or demo admin-token context

remaining_operations_blockers: production PostgreSQL operating model (topology, pooling, permissions, failover)
automated backup scheduling, restore RTO/RPO, DR failover
external observability, alerting, paging, dashboards, SLOs, log retention
staffed incident response program, exercises, playbooks
distributed abuse/rate-limit controls and operational tuning
secret/key rotation automation, KMS/HSM lifecycle, break-glass controls
workspace enforcement and full tenant lifecycle operations
retention/deletion/redaction, legal hold, audit export approval workflows

track_a_external_review_public_snapshot.goal: Enable bounded external review and prepare a future public snapshot candidate safely
steps: A1: Public Export Candidate Safety Scan — draft export candidate + full safety scan + explicit human approval
A2: Reviewer Handoff Pack — claim-safe reviewer instructions; synthetic/demo data only; security reports routing confirmed
A3: No-Real-Data Enforcement — runtime check before any non-demo path
B1: OAuth/JWT/OIDC Token Validation — replace static admin token; audit; fail-closed
B2: Secret Rotation Lifecycle — KMS/HSM or equivalent rotation design; break-glass
B3: External IAM Integration Baseline — SAML/SSO design (design only)

track_c_production_infrastructure.goal: Resolve PB-003, PB-004, PB-005, PB-009 infrastructure gaps
steps: C1: TLS/Container Hardening — TLS termination, container hardening, production CORS allowlist
C2: External Observability Stack — external log aggregation, metrics, alerting, dashboards, SLOs
C3: Backup/Restore/DR Automation — automated backup scheduling, restore RTO/RPO, DR failover exercise
C4: Production PostgreSQL Operating Model — topology, pooling, permissions, capacity, failover
D1: Workspace Enforcement — workspace_id enforcement, workspace-scoped queries
D2: Database Row-Level Security — PostgreSQL RLS implementation
D3: Tenant Provisioning API — tenant lifecycle management and operations

track_e_compliance_legal.goal: Resolve PB-011, PB-012, PB-006 compliance and data governance gaps
steps: E1: Compliance Gap Assessment — GDPR, SOC2, ISO gap analysis (assessment only)
E2: Legal / DPA — data processing agreement, legal review, retention/deletion policy
E3: Retention/Deletion/Redaction — production retention schedules, legal holds, deletion workflows

findings: GL-214 narrowly reduced PB-001 by constraining operator role vocabulary and adding durable operator audit events; the core static admin token P0 gap remains.
GL-215 narrowly reduced PB-002 by hardening secondary route tenant derivation and denying cross-tenant mutation on the demo path; workspace enforcement, RLS, and tenant lifecycle gaps remain.
GL-216 documented eleven production operations gaps without resolving any; all remain open P0/P1 blockers.
GL-216 post-merge suite shows 0 real regressions; 43 failures and 3 errors are known baseline scope-guard false positives.
Developer Preview and controlled external technical review (with strict boundaries) remain the correct operating tier.
Production SaaS, real customer data, private grant/institutional data, official SDK/package, compliance certification, and live PostgreSQL production readiness remain NO-GO.
Public snapshot preparation requires a separate gate and explicit human approval; public publish is DEFER/NO-GO.
recommended_next_issues: GL-218: Track A1 — Public Export Candidate Safety Scan (conditional; separate explicit approval required)
GL-219: Track B1 — Production IAM / OAuth/JWT Token Validation
GL-221: Track C2 — External Observability Stack
